name: Meta Platforms
headline: Meta Platforms (Fortune 500 PARENT entity, distinct from Meta AI in ai-labs) DOWNGRADE proposed: 10.9 → 9.4 (-1.5); Critical band sustained. Assesses the parent corporate entity for the May 20 8,000-person layoff (announced on or around May 16, executes May 20). Distinct from Meta AI (ai-labs, composite 26.3 after May 15 downgrade) which captures AI-product regulatory and child-safety dimensions. The May 20 layoff (8,000 workers + 6,000 cancelled requisitions = 14,000 effective headcount reduction) is the third 2026 layoff wave (January Reality Labs ~1,000-1,500; March 700; May 8,000) framed as AI-pivot restructuring concurrent with $115-135B AI infrastructure spend. Severance (16 weeks base + 2 weeks/year + 18 months health coverage) is sector-leading and materially better than Oracle's package — partial mitigating EMP/INT signal. Proposed dimensional changes: EMP -0.1 (third-wave displacement pattern documented, partially mitigated by severance); SYS -0.1 (multi-entity AI-pivot-displacement-wave sector pattern now systems-level Meta organizational signal). Other dimensions sustained. Composite: 10.9 → 9.4 / Critical sustained. Confidence MEDIUM-LOW: third-wave pattern is structurally documented but dimensional weight assignment is at assessor discretion given the partial mitigating severance signal.
indexPresence: Meta Platforms is present in fortune-500.json at rank 446 with composite 10.9. Distinct from Meta AI in ai-labs.json at rank 43 (composite 26.3 after May 15 downgrade).

AWR.rationale: Sustained. Detection infrastructure operational; May 20 layoff was planned and communicated; awareness present.

EMP.rationale: DOWNGRADE -0.1. Third 2026 layoff wave indicates systemic displacement-as-strategy pattern. Severance quality (16 weeks base + 2 weeks/year + 18 months health coverage) is materially better than Oracle's 4+1 weeks capped at 26 — partial mitigating signal prevents larger downgrade. Sub-step downgrade reflects third-wave pattern documentation against partial mitigating severance quality.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. Severance package quality is responsive to displacement need; 18 months health coverage is sector-leading proportionality signal. Mitigating against the SYS-pattern downgrade — holding ACT steady.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. No documented algorithmic-targeting allegations (contrast Oracle); selection-criterion documentation absent.

ACC.rationale: Sustained at floor. EU DSA preliminary breach finding (Meta AI scope) and Meta rejection of Commission findings on May 16 are AI-product accountability events scored under the Meta AI entity, not the Meta Platforms parent.

SYS.rationale: DOWNGRADE -0.1. S2 long-term-impact: third-wave AI-pivot displacement (46,750+ workers eliminated by Meta + Oracle + Microsoft in 50 days) is now a multi-entity industry pattern; Meta's contribution (8,000) is the largest single tranche of the May segment. Pattern indicates systems-level structural displacement-as-strategy across the AI-pivot Fortune 500 cluster. Sub-step downgrade reflects the multi-entity systems-pattern documentation.

INT.rationale: Sustained. Simultaneous $115-135B AI infrastructure spend + 14,000-person headcount reduction is internal-consistency tension, but sector-leading severance quality (18 months health coverage) is mitigating I3 internal-consistency signal. Net INT signal neutral; holding.

evidence.summary: Variety May 2026: Meta announces 8,000 employee layoffs effective May 20 2026 (10% of 78,865-person workforce); plus 6,000 cancelled open requisitions (14,000 effective headcount reduction).
TheNextWeb May 2026: Meta layoffs May 2026 — AI restructuring; reorganization into AI-focused 'pods' while spending $115-135B on AI infrastructure.
CNN April 23 2026: Meta layoffs 10% staff in AI restructuring — third wave following January (~1,000-1,500 Reality Labs) and March (700 across five divisions).
Severance package: 16 weeks base pay + 2 weeks per year of service + 18 months health coverage. Materially better than Oracle's 4+1 weeks capped at 26.

Sector pattern: Oracle (30,000 March 31) + Microsoft (8,750 voluntary buyout May 7) + Meta (8,000 announced May 16, effective May 20) = 46,750+ workers in 50 days across three Fortune-500 entities. All framed as AI-pivot restructuring.

keyEvidence: May 20: 8,000 layoffs effective (announced ~May 16); 6,000 cancelled requisitions = 14,000 effective headcount reduction.
Third 2026 layoff wave: January Reality Labs ~1,000-1,500; March 700; May 8,000.
Concurrent: $115-135B AI infrastructure spend; reorganization into AI-focused pods.
Severance: 16 weeks + 2 weeks/year + 18 months health coverage — sector-leading proportionality.
Sector context: Meta + Oracle + Microsoft = 46,750+ in 50 days.
Net dimensions: EMP -0.1, SYS -0.1 → composite 10.9 → 9.4 / Critical sustained.
rationale: Proposed -1.5 composite change reflects third-wave AI-pivot displacement pattern (EMP -0.1) and multi-entity systems-pattern documentation (SYS -0.1). Sector-leading severance quality (18 months health coverage) is a mitigating signal that prevents larger downgrades — Meta is treating displaced workers materially better than Oracle even while executing larger absolute headcount reductions over 2026. Proposed 9.4 keeps Meta Platforms in the Critical band. Math: (1.4+1.1+1.4+1.2+1.6+1.0+1.7+1.6)/8 = 11.0/8 = 1.375 → ((1.375-1)/4)*100 = 9.375 → 9.4.
watches: May 20 execution: confirm actual layoff count, severance, affected region breakdown
EU DSA preliminary breach finding response trajectory (Meta AI scope)
Meta AI (ai-labs) cycle interaction — keep entities scoped separately
carryForward: Severance-package quality as sector-relative INT/EMP positive signal
Distinct scoping: Meta Platforms (fortune-500) vs Meta AI (ai-labs)
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology observation: same parent organization (Meta) is scored under two indexes (Fortune 500 and AI Labs) for different conduct surfaces — layoff event properly scored under Fortune 500 parent entity (workforce dimension); AI-product regulatory events properly scored under AI Labs entity. The 'parent-vs-product-entity' scoping distinction is methodologically important and should be formalized: when an organization has multiple-index presence, conduct events should be assigned to the index whose dimensional scope is most relevant. Workforce events → Fortune 500; AI-product safety events → AI Labs.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Composite 9.4 sits within Critical band (0-20).

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. -1.5 composite delta from EMP -0.1 and SYS -0.1 net change. Critical band sustained.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
